---
title: "Codex-Author"
subtitle: |
  The Clo-Author for Codex  
  AI Research Architecture for Empirical Economics and other Social Science
---

An open-source Codex-native workflow for empirical economics and other social science. This repository is the template-first Codex port of Clo-Author and remains a work in progress while the migration from the Claude-era architecture is being tightened. It turns a repository into a structured research workspace with plans on disk, worker-critic quality control, domain calibration, and submission-oriented outputs.

**Canonical upstream:** [hugosantanna/clo-author](https://github.com/hugosantanna/clo-author)  
**This guide:** the live Codex port in this repository, where the active control plane is `AGENTS.md` plus `plugins/clo-author/`.

## Start in 5 Minutes

### 1. Fork and clone this Codex port

```bash
gh repo fork AnthonyPuggs/codex-author --clone
cd codex-author
```

Use this repository as the runtime surface. Treat `hugosantanna/clo-author` as the canonical upstream baseline for infrastructure comparisons, release checks, and selective refresh work.

### 2. Open the repo in Codex

Use your preferred Codex environment from the project root.

### 3. Run the activation smoke test

Before relying on natural-language skill routing, run the activation smoke test from `plugins/clo-author/references/runtime-activation.md`. The local marketplace entry is useful, but it is not sufficient evidence that the live Codex skill surface has actually loaded the Clo-Author bundle in the current session.

On this Codex harness, the reliable machine-level fallback is:

```bash
bash scripts/install_codex_skill_link.sh
```

Confirm in a fresh session that:

- `~/.agents/skills/clo-author` points to this repo's skill bundle
- the 10 skill names are present

If that check fails, treat the runtime as blocked and use `AGENTS.md` plus the active references as the fallback control plane until the plugin is loaded.

### 4. Start with `AGENTS.md`

Prompt Codex with:

> Read `AGENTS.md` and help me initialize this repository for a new empirical project in **[YOUR FIELD]** on **[YOUR TOPIC]**. Build the research spec first, then start a literature review on **[YOUR TOPIC]**.

The placeholders in `AGENTS.md` are intentional until that bootstrap step. This repository ships as a template first and becomes a working project when you fill them in.

### 5. Work in plain English

- "Review the literature on hospital consolidation."
- "Find the best public datasets for teacher pay and mobility."
- "Design the identification strategy for this policy shock."
- "Peer review this paper for JHR."

## What Changed From the Claude Version

- `AGENTS.md` replaces `CLAUDE.md`
- repo-local Codex skills replace slash commands
- file-backed memory replaces built-in hidden memory
- saved plans and session logs replace compaction-hook assumptions
- legacy `.claude/` files are kept only as migration source material
- active scoring, logging, and content standards live under `plugins/clo-author/references/`
- runtime activation now has an explicit smoke test instead of being assumed

## The Core Workflow

1. Discovery
2. Strategy
3. Execution
4. Review
5. Submission

Each phase can be entered directly. The active skill surface lives under `plugins/clo-author/skills/`.

## What the Plugin Provides

- `new-project` for end-to-end kickoff
- `discover` for literature, data, and ideation
- `strategize` for identification design
- `analyze` for scripts, tables, and figures
- `write` for manuscript drafting
- `review` for code review and simulated peer review
- `revise`, `talk`, `submit`, and `tools` for the rest of the pipeline

See the [User Guide](user-guide.qmd), [Agents](agents.qmd), [Architecture](architecture.qmd), [Customization](customization.qmd), and [Reference](reference.qmd).
